links.targetTitle: Swarm feed topics follow ph:v2:<category>:<scope> naming convention

global.title: Swarm manifests form a three-tier hierarchy of user, drive, and document feeds keyed by deterministic keccak256 topics.
global.content:
## Three-tier structure

### 1. User Manifest
- Topic: `keccak256("ph:v2:user:" + ownerAddress)`
- One per user, keyed by Ethereum address.
- Contents: `{ address, drives: { [driveId]: { name, preferredEditor, documentIds, lastUpdated } }, documents, stamps }`.
- Acts as the discovery index used on recovery.

### 2. Drive Manifest
- Topic: `keccak256("ph:v2:drive:" + driveId)`
- One per drive.
- Contents: `{ driveId, name, preferredEditor, documents: { [docId]: { documentType, name, parentFolder? } }, folders: { [folderId]: { name, parentFolder } } }`.
- Lets recovery reconstruct folder topology without replaying ops first.

### 3. Document Manifest
- Topic: `keccak256("ph:v2:doc:" + docId)`
- One per document.
- Contents: `{ documentId, documentType, operationBatches: [{ reference, scope, startIndex, endIndex }], latestRevision: { document, global }, keyframes }`.
- `reference` is the content hash of an encrypted `OperationWithContext[]` payload stored at `/bytes`.

### Deterministic topics

Because topics are hashes of stable strings, any client with the owner's address can find any drive without external coordination — the key to serverless recovery.
global.description: User manifest lists drives, drive manifest lists documents and folders, document manifest lists operation batch references. Each is a SOC feed with a deterministic topic.
